metadata.siteDescription: An illuminated scholarly archive: manuscripts, podcasts, and research on Medieval México.

footer.tagline: An illuminated scholarly archive: manuscripts, podcasts, and research on medieval Hispanic worlds.
footer.newsletterBlurb: Occasional updates on new digitized material and episodes.
footer.copyright: © MMXXIV México Medieval. An Illuminated Scholarly Archive.

home.heroImgAlt: Illuminated manuscript with gold leaf and gothic calligraphy on aged parchment
home.heroSub: Traversing three centuries of history through the manuscripts, melodies, and monumental works of Medieval México.
home.booksDesc: Annotated primary texts and rare first editions from the New Spain press.
home.articlesDesc: Scholarly critiques on ecclesiastical architecture and colonial governance.
home.podcastDesc: Discussions with historians specializing in the syncretism of Medieval México.
home.videosDesc: Cinematic tours of forgotten ruins and visual analyses of cathedral facades.
home.teachingDesc: Curricula and pedagogical resources for medievalists in the digital age.
home.spotlightImgAlt: Colonial monastery cloisters with sunbeams through stone arches
home.spotlightBody: Unearthed from the dusty shelves of the Franciscan archives, this collection chronicles the daily lives and spiritual synthesis of the Maya and the Spanish friars during the pivotal years of the 16th century.

libros.heroAlt: Ancient parchment manuscript with illuminated initials
libros.heroSub: Journey through the illuminated annals of México. From sacred liturgies to the maps that defined New Spain, explore the ink and spirit of five centuries.

articulos.subtitle: A curated selection of peer-reviewed research, journal entries, and historical chapters exploring the medieval foundations of New Spain.
articulos.journalExcerpt: Research article published in a specialized journal or academic proceedings.
articulos.chapterExcerpt: Peer-reviewed academic chapter in a collective volume.
articulos.chapterSource: In: The Making of Medieval Sardinia (Brill)

podcastPage.cardAlt1: Medieval knight armor and sword on velvet
podcastPage.cardAlt2: Parchment scroll with wax seal on wooden table
podcastPage.heroSub: Our podcast series explores the profound connections between the medieval Mediterranean and the early modern Americas. We translate scholarly rigor into narrative journeys through time.
podcastPage.collaborationsBody: Appearances on other podcasts and series: medieval historiography and outreach from México.

papers.title: Papers & presentations
papers.subtitle: Academic presentations at conferences and events, and papers in scholarly repositories.

videos.title: Videos
videos.subtitle: Lectures, appearances, and curated moving-image resources on medieval México and related scholarship.
videos.emptyBody: The video archive is not published yet. This page will list talks and recordings when they are ready—no broken links, just an empty shelf for now.

about.title: About us
about.subtitle: History, research, and outreach from an archive that honors the historian’s craft and the reach of contemporary medieval studies.
about.projectLede: México Medieval is a digital archive dedicated to medieval history in dialogue with México and the Hispanic world: academic research, curated materials, and outreach for readers inside and outside the university.
about.pillar1Body: Books, peer-reviewed articles, and papers exploring nobility, institutions, and narrative sources in the medieval Mediterranean and their historiographic echoes.
about.pillar2Body: The podcast and audio collaborations bridge erudite archives and wide conversation about myths, symbols, and medieval memory.
about.pillar3Body: This site gathers paths to books, articles, presentations, and episodes; other sections (for example video) will grow as they are published.
about.authorTagline: Historian and medievalist; his work combines prosopography, network analysis in narrative sources, and debates on Norman and south Italian nobility.
about.authorBio1: The editorial line of México Medieval reflects years of research on political society, counties, and sources of the Norman Mezzogiorno, as well as historiographic studies that question Mediterranean “connectivity” seen from the periphery and the Global South.
about.authorBio2: Beyond written scholarship, he advances audio outreach: the México Medieval series and appearances on other academic and cultural programs.
about.theme1: Kingdoms and nobility in medieval Italy

newsletter.title: The Archivist's Circle
newsletter.description: Join our exclusive circle of scholars and enthusiasts to receive monthly illuminated dispatches and early access to the vaults.
